resume:
Devon Mraz — Senior Growth Marketing Manager

CORE COMPETENCIES
Growth Marketing | Performance Marketing | Demand Generation | Paid Search | Paid Social | SEM | SEO | CRO | Marketing Automation | Lifecycle Marketing | Email Marketing | Funnel Optimization | Attribution Modeling | LTV/CAC | ROAS | MMM | A/B Testing | Multivariate Testing | HubSpot | Marketo | Salesforce | Google Ads | Meta Ads | TikTok Ads | LinkedIn Ads | GA4 | Looker | SQL | Segment | Braze | Iterable | Account-Based Marketing | PLG | Funnel Analytics | Cohort Analysis | Budget Management | Cross-functional Leadership | Stakeholder Management | OKRs | KPIs | Brand | Content | Influencer | Affiliate | Partnerships

SUMMARY
Data-driven, results-oriented senior growth marketer with 8 years scaling B2B and B2C acquisition. Expert across paid, lifecycle, and CRO. Track record of efficient growth and full-funnel optimization.

EXPERIENCE
Lumen Health (B2C subscription, telehealth) — Senior Growth Marketing Manager (2021-present)
- Own paid acquisition and lifecycle for the consumer subscription business; manage a ~$9M annual media budget and a team of three.
- Lead full-funnel programs across Google, Meta, and TikTok plus lifecycle email/push via Braze.
- Partner with data science on incrementality testing and attribution.

Northpeak SaaS — Growth Marketing Manager (2018-2021)
- Ran demand generation and paid search for a mid-market B2B product; supported pipeline and MQL targets.
- Built the marketing-automation nurture program in Marketo.

SKILLS / TOOLS
Google Ads, Meta Ads Manager, TikTok Ads, GA4, Looker, SQL, Braze, HubSpot, Marketo, Segment, Excel/Sheets, basic Python.

EDUCATION
B.A., Economics.

METRICS I LIVE BY
CAC, LTV, ROAS, payback period, blended vs. paid CAC, contribution margin.
jd: Lumen Health is hiring a Senior Growth Marketing Manager to own paid acquisition and early lifecycle for our consumer subscription. You will manage a multimillion-dollar media budget, set channel strategy across paid search/social, and run incrementality and CRO experiments that move CAC and subscriber payback. We want a marketer who can tell a complete story about a campaign or test they ran — the situation and goal, exactly what they changed, and the quantified result with timeframe and a clear read on causality (incrementality, not last-click). You'll present performance to the VP of Marketing and partner with data science and finance on attribution. Requirements: 6+ years in growth/performance marketing, hands-on with paid platforms and GA4/SQL, and demonstrated ability to improve CAC or payback with evidence of incremental lift.

history.q: Your resume is a wall of tools and acronyms. Cut through it for me: tell me about one acquisition program you ran at Lumen where you actually moved CAC.
Walk me through it like a story — what was the situation and target, what exactly did you do, and what was the measured result?

candidate_last_answer: Situation: in Q1 2023 our blended CAC on paid had crept from about $58 to $74 over two quarters, mostly because Meta prospecting was saturating — frequency was up and incremental conversions were falling. Target the VP gave me was to get blended paid CAC back under $60 within the quarter without cutting subscriber volume. What I did: I cut the bottom 20% of Meta prospecting audiences by ROAS, shifted roughly $180K of that monthly spend into a TikTok Spark Ads test and into Google non-brand, and I ran a geo-based incrementality test for two weeks to confirm the TikTok spend was actually incremental rather than stealing organic. Result: over the quarter blended paid CAC came down to $56, the geo test showed TikTok was about 82% incremental, subscriber volume held within 3% of plan, and payback dropped from 11 months to 9. The one caveat is the incrementality read had wide confidence intervals because two weeks was short, so I kept the geo holdout running another month to firm it up.

top_question_traits: must-anchor-on: the incrementality read had wide confidence intervals because two weeks was short

expected-yield: how the candidate handled the weak-confidence incrementality signal — what they did to firm it up, how they decided to keep scaling TikTok despite wide intervals, and what threshold would have made them pull back — testing analytical rigor behind a strong STAR answer
